metric_value: De facto enterprise meta-directory candidate
notes: Aberdeen asserts NDS meets all four criteria: Extensible Scalable Portable Available — best of available directories.

metric_value: NDS to embrace LDAP as standards-based front-end to meta-directory
notes: Aberdeen predicts Novell will finalize LDAP integration once standard stabilized.

metric_value: NDS fully LDAP-enabled; eDirectory became leading LDAP directory server
notes: NDS renamed eDirectory ~2000 and became multi-platform LDAP directory. LDAPv3 standard approved December 1997.

metric_value: NDS for NT to be delivered mid-1997
notes: Novell stated NDS on NT ready summer 1997 — up to one year before Microsoft Active Directory.

metric_value: NDS for NT shipped 1997; Microsoft Active Directory shipped with Windows 2000 in February 2000
notes: Novell met its shipping target. Microsoft Active Directory was delayed substantially past the mid-1998 estimate Aberdeen cited.

metric_value: 75% of Unix servers shipping NDS by end of 1997
notes: Novell goal stated by Aberdeen. HP SunSoft SCO Caldera Siemens Nixdorf ICL Olivetti NCR Data General Unisys cited.

metric_value: 60 million NDS users by end of 1999 per IDC estimate
notes: IDC data cited in 1999 reports showed approximately 60 million NDS users despite Microsoft competition.

metric_value: NDS positioned to become de facto enterprise meta-directory standard
notes: Aberdeen concludes NDS has best match with Extensible/Scalable/Portable/Available criteria and largest market/mind share.

metric_value: Active Directory with Windows 2000 became dominant enterprise directory standard
notes: Windows 2000 released February 2000 with Active Directory. Despite initial slow uptake Active Directory became dominant by mid-2000s displacing NDS.

metric_value: Novell acquired by Attachmate for $2.2B in 2011
notes: Attachmate acquired Novell; Microsoft-led consortium paid $450M for Novell patents. NDS/eDirectory continued under successive owners.

metric_value: Active Directory announced but not available; mid-1998 target cited
notes: Aberdeen notes Microsoft's AD delay creates inertia impeding enterprise directory adoption — organizations wait rather than deploying NDS.

metric_value: LDAP consortium of 40+ vendors endorsed LDAP as internet directory protocol in April 1996
notes: LDAPv3 named PC Magazine Technical Excellence award winner November 1997; approved as proposed Internet standard December 1997.

metric_value: AT&T Deutsche Telekom NTT hosting NDS leads to 500000 new users in 1997
notes: Three largest ISPs cited. Demonstrates NDS internet presence strategy.

metric_value: Novell and CA announced ManageWise integration with Unicenter TNG for enterprise-wide management
notes: Integration provides end-to-end LAN-to-WAN coverage using NDS as common directory underpinning.

metric_value: NOS without strong directory services should not be considered for mid-to-large enterprise
notes: Aberdeen contention: only NDS IBM Warp Server and Banyan StreetTalk meet the criterion. Microsoft NT Server does not.

metric_value: Maintaining multiple non-synchronized directories is neither cost-effective nor efficient
notes: Aberdeen recommends single standards-based hierarchical directory replicated across platforms with LDAP front-ends to app-specific directories.

metric_value: LDAP-based directory consolidation became standard enterprise practice; AD became single-directory standard for Windows environments
notes: Aberdeen's recommendation for single hierarchical directory proved accurate; Active Directory fulfilled this role in Microsoft environments while LDAP directories served Unix/Linux.

metric_value: NDS single sign-on spanning multiple servers and applications with unified object-based user profile
notes: Dynamic directory updates visible in real-time; drag-and-drop directory management across multiple trees; mobile worldwide access.

metric_value: Oracle agreed to use NDS authentication and management on all shared platforms
notes: Significant ISV endorsement validating NDS as cross-platform directory standard.

metric_value: Enterprise directory services essential for VPN and e-commerce deployments over next 18 months
notes: IS and LOB executives uniformly describe plans for intranets VPNs and e-commerce requiring directory services infrastructure.
